## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On June 25, 1950, the incredibly massive, incredibly devastating, and highly consequential Korean War officially and aggressively commenced. Over 75,000 heavily armed troops of the North Korean People's Army, massively backed by the Soviet Union, aggressively surged across the 38th parallel to violently invade South Korea. This massive, unprovoked military invasion immediately triggered a massive, aggressive response from the United Nations and the United States, fundamentally escalating the Cold War into an absolute, bloody global proxy conflict.

On June 25, 1876, the absolute, most massive and heavily legendary defeat of the United States Army during the Indian Wars occurred at the Battle of the Little Bighorn. The heavily outnumbered 7th Cavalry Regiment, aggressively commanded by Lieutenant Colonel George Armstrong Custer, was completely and absolutely annihilated by a massive, highly coordinated coalition of Lakota Sioux, Northern Cheyenne, and Arapaho warriors. This incredibly massive Native American victory profoundly and permanently shocked the American public during its centennial celebrations.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On June 25, 1975, the incredibly massive, deeply horrific, and absolute darkest chapter of post-independence Indian democracy officially began. Prime Minister Indira Gandhi aggressively and controversially advised President Fakhruddin Ali Ahmed to heavily declare a massive state of National Emergency across India, citing internal security threats. This massive, draconian decree aggressively suspended all absolute civil liberties, heavily censored the press, and violently resulted in the massive, immediate imprisonment of thousands of political opponents across the republic.

On June 25, 1932, the incredibly massive, highly prestigious, and deeply foundational milestone of Indian sports was officially achieved. The Indian national cricket team aggressively played its absolute, very first official Test match at the legendary Lord's Cricket Ground in London against England. Led heavily by the formidable C. K. Nayudu, this massive international debut fundamentally and aggressively established India's absolute, permanent presence on the global cricketing stage, deeply fostering a massive national obsession that would unite the diverse subcontinent.
